global.title: The Latest: Presidents Xi Jinping and Donald Trump meet in Beijing for high-profile summit - Greenwich Time
global.summary: Trump and Xi have begun a high-stakes summit in Beijing with trade, Taiwan, technology, and the Iran war all on the table. Early expectations are modest, but the meeting could shape global stability and economic confidence.
global.whyItMatters: Pastors should watch moments like this because geopolitical tensions quickly affect congregants through anxiety, prices, and prayer needs.

global.title: World shares mostly gain as investors watch Trump-Xi summit - Greenwich Time
global.summary: Markets rose as investors responded to signals from the Trump-Xi meeting, including agreement that the Strait of Hormuz must be reopened. The story reflects how closely the global economy is tied to war and diplomacy.
global.whyItMatters: Economic uncertainty touches family budgets, benevolence needs, and the emotional climate in a congregation.

global.title: Morning Bid: AI rally powers on, Trump-Xi summit takes centre stage - Reuters
global.summary: Reuters says investors are balancing AI-driven optimism with concern over the Iran war and political instability in Britain. The report highlights how fragile confidence remains even amid market gains.
global.whyItMatters: This is a useful snapshot for preaching and leadership: people are living with mixed signals of prosperity, fear, and fatigue.

canadian.title: Mining company responsible for drilling in sacred site withdrawals operations - APTN News
canadian.summary: A mining company has withdrawn plans tied to drilling near a sacred site after legal action and advocacy from Sioux Nation tribes. The development highlights the ongoing importance of Indigenous voices and protection of sacred land.
canadian.whyItMatters: For a pastor in Canada, this connects to reconciliation, respect for sacred spaces, and listening well to Indigenous communities.

local.title: Power outage planned in downtown Summerland - Summerland Review
local.summary: A planned overnight power outage from May 14 to 15 will affect parts of downtown Summerland, including Henry Avenue, Jubilee Road, and Main Street. The utility says timing could change if crews are needed for an emergency.
local.whyItMatters: This is practical local awareness for church operations, member care, and checking in on seniors or vulnerable neighbours affected by the outage.

persecutedChurch.title: Blessed Sacrament stolen from Nigerian adoration chapel - Catholic World Report
persecutedChurch.summary: A report on the theft of the Blessed Sacrament in Nigeria also underscores the wider crisis facing Christians there, including escalating attacks by armed groups and renewed concern from the U.S. Commission on International Religious Freedom.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a clear call to prayer, informed advocacy, and solidarity with believers facing severe violence for their faith.

persecutedChurch.title: Ihab Hassan: ‘No accountability’ over attacks on Palestinian Christians - Vatican News
persecutedChurch.summary: Palestinian Christian advocate Ihab Hassan says settler attacks on Christians in the West Bank continue with little accountability, and he is urging global believers to show solidarity through the new 'Save West Bank Christians' initiative.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It helps pastors lead informed prayer for vulnerable Christian communities and remember believers often overlooked in wider conflict coverage.

leadership.title: Why This CPO Believes AI and Emotional Intelligence Must Work Together - Forbes
leadership.summary: This piece argues that while AI can accelerate work, it cannot care, build trust, or make people feel seen. The central leadership insight is that human presence and emotional intelligence remain essential in an AI-shaped workplace.
leadership.whyItMatters: For church leadership, it is a timely reminder that ministry effectiveness still depends on empathy, discernment, and relational care.

goodNews.title: Deaf Rescue Dog Sees the World & Life Outside a Cage for the First Time - DogTime
goodNews.summary: After three years in a shelter cage, a deaf rescue dog named Milkshake has been adopted and is experiencing safety, freedom, and family for the first time. It is a small but moving picture of patience and restoration.
goodNews.whyItMatters: Stories of rescue and gentleness can offer a refreshing pastoral illustration of hope, dignity, and new beginnings.
